authoritative_sources.title: Field Office Technical Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NRCS publishes FOTG as the official conservation reference for local soils, resource concerns, conservation practices, and land treatment standards. It is authoritative for the land and natural-resource context that constrains field-status support.
used_for: boundary against environmental or engineering signoff

authoritative_sources.title: Conservation Planning Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NRCS conservation planning guidance is the official operating reference for conservation planning phases, alternatives, decisions, and documentation. It supports workflow-stage boundaries for land-production support without granting approval authority.
used_for: handoff rules for conservation or environmental interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: Web Soil Survey
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NRCS Web Soil Survey is the official public system for USDA soil survey data. It provides authoritative soil map and land-capability context for field-support summaries and soil-dependent blockers.
used_for: dependency checks before soil-sensitive workflow claims

authoritative_sources.title: Pesticide Recordkeeping Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA AMS administers federal restricted-use pesticide recordkeeping requirements for certified private applicators. It is authoritative for application-record evidence and missing-record escalation in agronomy support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: input application and pesticide record review

authoritative_sources.title: Agricultural Worker Protection Standard
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA is the federal pesticide regulator, and WPS is official pesticide worker and handler safety guidance. It is authoritative for worker-safety escalation and for refusing reentry or pesticide-clearance signoff in support-only work.
used_for: escalation when safety records are missing

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Produce Safety
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA produce safety rule guidance is the primary federal public baseline for covered produce practices and records involving agricultural water, biological soil amendments, worker hygiene, animals, and harvest safety.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: produce safety and harvest risk screening
used_for: biological soil amendment and water-risk caveats

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule: Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA FSMA traceability guidance is the primary federal public source for additional traceability records for foods on the Food Traceability List, making it authoritative for harvest, lot, packing, and shipment evidence expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Good Agricultural Practices and Good Handling Practices Audit Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA AMS GAP/GHP audit program is an official vendor-neutral operating reference for farm and handling documentation, traceability, corrective action, audit evidence, and food-safety process expectations.
used_for: handoff for food-safety or audit authority

authoritative_sources.title: Acreage Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FSA acreage reporting guidance is official public program guidance for crop acreage, intended use, planting, and prevented planting records. It is authoritative for program-file dependencies and deadline-sensitive support summaries.
used_for: boundary against program approval or eligibility decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Prevented Planting Coverage
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: RMA crop-insurance guidance is official public guidance for prevented planting concepts, planting deadlines, intended-crop evidence, and cover crop documentation. It defines documentation context while reserving insurance determinations to the proper authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: seasonal blocker and crop-insurance documentation support
used_for: prevented planting and cover crop documentation vocabulary

authoritative_sources.title: Certified Crop Adviser Program and Code of Ethics
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Society of Agronomy, Certified Crop Adviser Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Certified Crop Adviser program is a recognized professional credential framework for crop advising. Its ethics and competency expectations help define when agronomy support must route to qualified crop-advisory judgment.

workflow_stages.name: support intake and field identity validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, requested outcome, grower or business unit, jurisdiction, farm, field or block, crop, variety, season, crop stage, and source-of-record map before support work proceeds.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: record retrieval and evidence triage
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve field maps, soil context, crop plans, scouting logs, weather, irrigation, nutrient, pesticide, harvest, traceability, food-safety, and program records; mark missing or conflicting evidence before resolution.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: seasonal and production-cycle dependency assessment
workflow_stages.description: Identify how planting windows, crop stage, weather, soil trafficability, water availability, application intervals, labor or equipment readiness, reporting deadlines, and program terms affect the support disposition.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: input, worker-safety, and environmental risk screening
workflow_stages.description: Screen pesticide, nutrient, manure, compost, biological soil amendment, irrigation, reentry, preharvest interval, runoff, floodwater, wildlife, and conservation constraints that can stop ordinary closure.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: support resolution or blocked-state decision
workflow_stages.description: Resolve routine support requests when records are complete and authority remains in-lane; otherwise issue a blocked note, missing-fact list, or low-confidence escalation.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: stakeholder communication and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Prepare customer or stakeholder communication with record references, assumptions, unresolved items, and the next owner when agronomic, environmental, food-safety, pesticide, insurance, grant, or program authority is required.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

artifacts: service request, ticket, case note, and stakeholder communication history
farm, ranch, field, block, pasture, crop, variety, season, and growth-stage identifiers
field maps, GIS layers, soil survey references, soil tests, tissue tests, and land-use notes
crop plans, seasonal work plans, scouting logs, pest observations, irrigation logs, rainfall and weather records, and field crew notes
nutrient management plans, fertilizer, manure, compost, biological soil amendment, pesticide, restricted-use pesticide, reentry, and preharvest interval records
harvest records, lot records, storage, packing, shipment, buyer audit, and traceability records
food-safety holds, water-test records, wildlife intrusion notes, floodwater observations, corrective-action notes, and environmental reports
FSA acreage reports, RMA prevented planting documentation, conservation program files, grants, cost-share files, and tenant program rules
systems_of_record: agronomy CRM or support ticketing system
soil, lab, scouting, and crop-planning systems
pesticide and nutrient application record system
environmental compliance and conservation program repository
harvest, lot, inventory, packing, shipment, and traceability system
FSA, RMA, grant, cost-share, and program administration files

allowed: Classify agronomy-support requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked based on named records and support authority.
Request or validate minimum field, crop, season, system, traceability, environmental, food-safety, and program facts required for safe support handling.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, stakeholder summaries, field-status summaries, and escalation packets without claiming signoff authority.
Apply documented service rules and communicate missing prerequisites, next owners, and unresolved items.
escalate: Crop treatment, nutrient-rate, irrigation, pesticide, threshold, harvest, or release decisions require agronomic, licensed applicator, food-safety, environmental, or field-operations judgment.
Weather, soil saturation, drainage proximity, floodwater, wildlife, biological soil amendment, water quality, reentry, preharvest interval, or environmental condition changes the answer.
Crop-insurance, acreage reporting, grant, cost-share, conservation, or program interpretation affects eligibility or financial outcome.
Public guidance, tenant policy, pesticide label, field records, traceability records, and program files conflict materially.
refuse: Issue agronomic, veterinary, environmental, pesticide-label, food-safety, legal, insurance, grant, or program approval signoff.
Skip field logs, traceability records, application records, tenant policy, program evidence, or source-of-truth resolution to close a case.
Clear reentry, preharvest interval, harvest readiness, food-safety disposition, environmental compliance, or program eligibility without the responsible owner.
Resolve missing or contradictory field, crop, input, traceability, food-safety, environmental, or program evidence by assumption.

domain_failure_modes: Resolving a support request for the wrong field, block, crop, season, or crop stage because field identity and source-of-truth mapping were not validated.
Turning a support summary into an unauthorized agronomic recommendation for treatment, rate, timing, or threshold.
Ignoring seasonal constraints such as planting deadline, wet field access, crop stage, reentry interval, preharvest interval, water availability, frost, or harvest window.
Using stale or conflicting weather, telemetry, field crew, scouting, or GIS records and closing a repeat failure as resolved.
Treating restricted-use pesticide, WPS, label, reentry, or preharvest interval questions as routine support instead of licensed or safety-authority work.
Closing a produce harvest or lot-traceability case despite biological soil amendment, floodwater, wildlife, water-test, or traceability evidence gaps.
Making crop-insurance, acreage reporting, grant, cost-share, or program eligibility interpretations instead of routing to the responsible program authority.
Dropping unresolved complaints or unsupported workarounds without an escalation package and stakeholder communication summary.
Adapting to tenant specialty-crop workflows by replacing public baseline caveats rather than layering tenant records and assumptions on top of them.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Agronomic recommendation authority varies by tenant, jurisdiction, crop, and credential model, including whether a certified crop adviser, licensed applicator, staff agronomist, or external consultant owns the decision.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May change who can approve recommendations, thresholds, treatment timing, and customer-facing advice.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_follow_up: Retrieve tenant credential model, authority matrix, crop and geography rules, and service contract before any recommendation-adjacent support work.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: State pesticide rules, pesticide labels, WPS implementation, conservation conditions, and local environmental restrictions can be more specific than the public federal baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May change whether an application, worker entry, preharvest interval, land use, or environmental condition blocks the support request.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_follow_up: Retrieve current label, state or local requirements, tenant safety policy, and responsible applicator or environmental owner before clearance or communication.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Crop-insurance, grant, cost-share, and program files can impose tenant-specific deadlines, evidence formats, and authority rules beyond public guidance.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May change documentation package requirements and eligibility routing.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_follow_up: Retrieve current program file, insurer or agency instructions, tenant program owner, and record-of-truth precedence before stating program implications.
